claim: Breaking complex problems into small iterative steps with immediate feedback (Understand Problem → Devise Plan → Carry Out Plan → Look Back and Reflect) significantly improves problem-solving effectiveness with AI assistance.
actionable: Apply the four-step Solveit methodology: understand the problem fully, devise a plan, execute incrementally with verification at each step, then reflect and extract lessons learned.
source_title: The antidote to AI fatigue — Answer.ai Solveit

claim: Dialogue engineering (controlling context and conversation flow) allows AI models to be more accurate and helpful than simple prompt engineering because the AI can see and build upon previous step corrections.
actionable: Use dialogue-based interfaces where you can edit AI responses in real time, branch conversations, and create collapsible sections to manage context, allowing the AI to learn from corrections without context degradation.
source_title: The antidote to AI fatigue — Answer.ai Solveit

claim: Auto-regressive language models degrade when asked to correct mistakes in conversation history; instead, you should be able to directly edit AI responses in the dialogue to guide the model without pollution.
actionable: When working with AI coding/writing assistants, avoid corrective prompts on mistakes; instead use interfaces that let you edit the AI's previous output directly, then continue the conversation.
source_title: The antidote to AI fatigue — Answer.ai Solveit

claim: Persistent Linux containers with individual VPS endpoints enable AI-assisted development with full system access, package installation, and persistent state without requiring deployment, making experimentation frictionless.
actionable: Use platforms (like Solveit) that provide ephemeral Linux containers with accessible URLs for each user session, enabling direct server testing and custom tool installation without separate deployment steps.
source_title: The antidote to AI fatigue — Answer.ai Solveit

claim: Learning mode for AI assistants should provide small, digestible steps to teach the user rather than generating large blocks of code, prioritizing user understanding and capability growth over output speed.
actionable: When using AI coding assistants, enable learning/teaching modes that provide step-by-step guidance with explanations over complete solutions, ensuring you understand and can maintain the code afterward.
source_title: The antidote to AI fatigue — Answer.ai Solveit

claim: Full context visibility in both directions (human seeing AI context, AI seeing human context) enables more effective collaboration and reduces errors that come from asymmetric information.
actionable: When designing AI collaboration tools, ensure both parties (human and AI) can see the same information, variables, outputs, and intermediate results at all times.
source_title: The antidote to AI fatigue — Answer.ai Solveit

claim: Live execution environment (code runs immediately in dialogue) with browsable results allows real-time error detection and fixing, making it more efficient than chat-based debugging.
actionable: Use AI development tools that execute code live in the same context window and display results immediately, allowing you to inspect outputs and fix issues before proceeding.
source_title: The antidote to AI fatigue — Answer.ai Solveit

claim: Composability of AI-generated modules as importable Python functions enables reuse across projects without formal software engineering overhead (no deployment, versioning, or API design needed).
actionable: Design AI development platforms so that completed modules or dialogues can be imported as Python modules directly from shared storage, enabling simple reuse by importing them like standard libraries.
source_title: The antidote to AI fatigue — Answer.ai Solveit

claim: The perception of AI code generation as a shortcut is misleading; vibe coding without understanding creates unmaintainable code, while iterative learning-focused development with AI produces superior long-term results.
actionable: Avoid letting AI write large code blocks without your understanding; instead use small-step approaches where you learn each piece, understand why it works, and maintain full comprehension of your codebase.
source_title: The antidote to AI fatigue — Answer.ai Solveit

claim: Longer dialogues with edited corrections improve AI performance (opposite of context degradation in traditional chat), because the AI learns from explicit human corrections embedded in conversation history.
actionable: In dialogue-based AI development, actively correct and improve AI outputs by editing them in place; longer conversations with corrections should improve quality over time if the interface supports this.
source_title: The antidote to AI fatigue — Answer.ai Solveit

claim: A 12-person constraint forces exceptional hiring and multidisciplinary generalists, enabling outsized productivity relative to company size through focus and exceptional human capital.
actionable: When building AI-native teams, prioritize hiring true multidisciplinary generalists who can go deep on many topics rather than specialists, and maintain small team sizes to force focused decision-making.
source_title: The antidote to AI fatigue — Answer.ai Solveit

claim: Fact-checking long documents in an AI requires working section-by-section rather than end-to-end because attention mechanisms struggle with maintaining accuracy across very long contexts.
actionable: When fact-checking long documents with AI, ask the model to verify sections independently rather than the entire document at once, combining results afterward.
source_title: The antidote to AI fatigue — Answer.ai Solveit

claim: Most knowledge about AI tool workflows comes from solving your own problems first, then productizing what works; this eat-your-own-dog-food approach reveals real user needs better than speculation.
actionable: When building AI tools, use them internally for your own workflows first (e.g., Answer.ai used Solveit for legal, accounting, and development), then refine based on real friction points.
source_title: The antidote to AI fatigue — Answer.ai Solveit
